(a) WRONG DOI: DOI resolves to Plaut 2008 Pertussis toxin retrograde transport paper, NOT SiiE Salmonella. Wrong reference. Real SiiE T1SS paper: Gerlach 2007 Cell Microbiol 9:1834 or Wagner 2014.; (b) family (SiiE (Salmonella SPI-4 giant adhesin)) is a validated T1SS substrate family per Linhartova 2010 (10.1111/j.1574-6976.2010.00231.x) and other reviews; cited DOI is wrong but family-level substrate identity is independently supported; (c) locus_tag present, not cross-verified against NCBI in this audit

quote: The two-partner secretion pathway in Gram-negative bacteria consists of a TpsA exoprotein and a cognate TpsB outer membrane translocator protein ... we characterized the functional domains of the Haemophilus influenzae HMW1B protein, a TpsB protein that interacts with the H. influenzae HMW1 adhesin.
uniprot_note: HMW2A (HMW2) = second high-molecular-weight adhesin, a TpsA exoprotein paralogous to HMW1A. TpsB partner is HMW2B (hmw2ABC operon). locus_tag R2846_1072 in strain R2846, chromosome NC_017452.1. Quote characterizes the HMW1/HMW1B TPS system; HMW2A is the well-established paralog secreted by its own HMW2B.
